description:
You are an atmospheric scientist at a climate research institute analyzing the NASA/GISS Surface Temperature Analysis (GISTEMP v4) global mean surface temperature anomaly record. The dataset is the global-mean monthly temperature anomaly series from NASA GISS (1880–present), available at https://data.giss.nasa.gov/gistemp/tabledata_v4/GLB.Ts+dSST.csv.

Your complete analysis must produce four deliverables:

1. Decompose the annual temperature anomaly time series (1880–2023) using STL decomposition (stats::stl()). Write the decomposition components to /home/ga/RProjects/output/climate_stl_components.csv with columns: year, observed, trend, seasonal, remainder. Use annual averages computed from the monthly data (columns Jan–Dec, skipping non-numeric entries like '***').

2. Fit an ARIMA model (auto.arima from the forecast package) to the trend component extracted from STL. Forecast 10 years ahead (2024–2033). Write forecasts to /home/ga/RProjects/output/climate_forecast.csv with columns: year, forecast, lower80, upper80, lower95, upper95.

3. Detect structural breakpoints in the annual trend component using the changepoint package (cpt.mean() or cpt.var()). Write breakpoint results to /home/ga/RProjects/output/climate_breakpoints.csv with columns: breakpoint_year, segment_mean_before, segment_mean_after.

4. Generate a 3-panel time series figure showing: (top) observed annual anomaly with trend overlay, (middle) ARIMA forecast with 80%/95% confidence bands extending to 2033, (bottom) changepoint detection visualization with segment means. Save to /home/ga/RProjects/output/climate_analysis.png.

Login: username=ga, password=password123. RStudio is open. Write your code in /home/ga/RProjects/climate_analysis.R. The GISTEMP dataset has already been downloaded to /home/ga/RProjects/datasets/gistemp_global.csv.

metadata.dataset: NASA GISTEMP v4 global mean surface temperature anomaly (1880-2023)
metadata.expected_breakpoints: at least one breakpoint (likely 1975-1985 rapid warming onset)
metadata.task_note: Uses NASA GISTEMP real data; forecast + changepoint packages needed
